source_metadata.title_raw: Cassandra M. Ferrannini - A-18-009(a) - March 27, 2018 - Sacramento

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
This Ferrannini Advice Letter No. 18-009(a) rescinds Ferrannini Advice Letter, No. A-18-009 
Cassandra M. Ferrannini 
Downey Brand LLP 
621 Capitol Mall, 18th Floor 
Sacramento, CA 95814 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-18-009(a) 
Dear Ms. Ferrannini: 
March 27, 2018 
By vote on March 22, 2018, the Commission has determined that your question, on behalf 
of Senator Tony Mendoza, presents issues requiring a policy interpretation best made through a 
Commission Opinion under Government Code Section 83 l 14(a) of the Political Reform Act.1 (See 
Regulation I8329(b)(8)(E).) Accordingly, the Commission withdraws and rescinds the staff issued 
Ferrannini Advice Letter, No. A-18-009, dated February 20, 2018, in its entirety. 
This letter serves to notify Senator Mendoza the he should no longer act in reliance of the 
previously issued advice, and any immunity pursuant to Section 83114(b) no longer applies to 
actions he takes after receipt of this letter. If Senator Mendoza would like his question further 
considered, Senator Mendoza should request a Commission Opinion.2 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
Sincerely, 
Brian G. Lau 
Assistant General Counsel 
BGL:jgl 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code ofRegulations. All 
regulatory references arc to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 
2 This letter supersedes the Ferrannini Advice Letter, No. A-18-009, dated February 20, 2018.

sections.question_synthetic: The original advice letter A-18-009 addressed a legal question on behalf of Senator Tony Mendoza regarding Political Reform Act compliance; this withdrawal letter does not restate the original question.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC Commission voted to withdraw and rescind the previously issued Ferrannini Advice Letter A-18-009 (dated February 20, 2018) in its entirety, determining that the matter presents policy interpretation issues requiring a Commission Opinion under Government Code Section 83114(a) rather than staff advice, and Senator Mendoza should no longer rely on the prior advice or its immunity protections.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Withdrawal letter with no QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. Original question content not included in this withdrawal document. Document clearly identifies itself as rescinding prior advice letter A-18-009 dated February 20, 2018.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC Commission voted to withdraw and rescind the previously issued Ferrannini Advice Letter A-18-009 (dated February 20, 2018) in its entirety, determining that the matter presents policy interpretation issues requiring a Commission Opinion under Government Code Section 83114(a) rather than staff advice, and Senator Mendoza should no longer rely on the prior advice or its immunity protections.

embedding.summary: This is a withdrawal letter rescinding FPPC Advice Letter A-18-009 regarding Senator Tony Mendoza's question. The Commission determined the matter requires policy interpretation through a formal Commission Opinion rather than staff advice.
